level.1.name: KX-7: First Contact
level.1.subtitle: Deploy your first tower against the Void Swarm
level.1.briefing:
Stellar Perimeter -- Mission Briefing

================================

[Background: Stellar Era 2847]

In the year 2847, the United Federation's territory has expanded across dozens of star systems. Frontier outposts, like scattered fireflies in the deep void, maintain the Federation's control and resource extraction in the far reaches of space. Behind this seemingly peaceful frontier, a silent catastrophe has been brewing.

It all began at the edge of a star system designated KX-7.

First, communications went dark. Then supply ships stopped reporting. Finally, a fully-equipped deep-space survey fleet, after jumping to the system, transmitted only an 11-second video clip -- in the darkness, entities of twisted metal and organic matter were pouring out from a torn spatial rift. They possessed no fixed form, yet harbored an instinct to devour all metallic structures.

Federation archives named this unknown threat the "Void Swarm".

================================

[Swarm Characteristics]

The Swarm are not biological entities in the traditional sense. They are essentially projections of higher-dimensional consciousness cast into lower dimensions -- each "individual" flows like black liquid metal, constantly recombining, fragmenting, and regrouping as it moves. The naked eye cannot capture their true outline; one moment a twisted worm-like aggregate, the next scattering into hundreds of violet-glowing droplets.

Yet the Swarm are not entirely unobservable. When passing through spatial rifts, they emit an extremely faint subspace harmonic wave. Federation scientists urgently retrofitted deep-space mineral prospecting equipment -- the "Resonance Locator" -- cranking its sensitivity up by three orders of magnitude, allowing them to precisely capture Swarm signals on star charts and mark their movement trajectories and clustering hotspots as red arrows.

The arrow-marked enemy targets you see on your tactical viewport are not the Swarm's true forms, but tactical symbols translated from invisible threats by the Resonance Locator into human-readable data.

================================

[The Core Conflict: Stellar Core Orbs]

The Swarm's sole purpose in swarming human outposts is to seize a condensed energy source known as the "Stellar Core Orb".

Stellar Core Orbs are ultra-high-density energy carriers extracted and compressed from galactic cores by the outposts -- a fist-sized orb contains enough energy to power an entire city for a decade. To the Swarm, these orbs are not merely sustenance, but "fuel" for tearing open space and expanding the rifts. Every orb seized means the rift grows wider, allowing more Swarm to pour in from the void depths.

Defense systems, communications arrays, life support -- every system on the outpost depends on Stellar Core Orbs. Lose the orbs, and you lose everything.

However, the Swarm's hunger for orbs is also their weakness. Building on the Resonance Locator, Federation scientists developed the "Harmonic Trajectory Array." As the Swarm pass through rifts and move toward energy cores, they leave behind extremely faint subspace harmonic residuals. The Array captures these residual signals to not only mark the Swarm's real-time positions on the star chart, but also reverse-engineer their optimal travel routes -- the golden path lines you see in the world viewport.

These path lines are a mathematical manifestation of the Swarm's "instinct": they always choose the shortest route to the nearest energy source. And we can use that against them, building killing grounds along their inevitable path.

================================

[Your Mission]

You have been appointed as an emergency Federation Defense Engineer, dispatched to outpost platforms across multiple star systems to deploy automated defense arrays on limited alloy decks, holding back the Swarm's spread.

Your first assignment is the fallen Outpost KX-7. Its automated defenses have been destroyed, leaving only a few alloy platforms and surviving energy cores. Swarm advance forces are closing in.

================================

[Mission Objectives: Outpost KX-7]

- Deploy Machine Gun tower arrays on the outpost's glowing-grid alloy decks
- Use Resonance Locators to track Swarm movement and destroy them before they reach the base core
- Protect all 5 remaining Stellar Core Orbs
- If all orbs are lost, the outpost will lose power and fall permanently

[Tip]
Click the "MG Tower" button on the right panel, then click an appropriate glowing grid cell on the map to deploy. Machine Gun towers will automatically attack Swarm units that enter their range.

================================

This starfield is beautiful. But beautiful things are often the most dangerous.
level.2.name: Winding Defense
level.2.subtitle: Master upgrades and path coverage
level.2.briefing:
Stellar Perimeter -- Mission Briefing

================================

[Background: Crisis in the Deep Decks]

The surface defenses of Outpost KX-7 have been temporarily stabilized under your command. But deep-scan readings from the Resonance Locator show that the Swarm has not retreated — they are penetrating deeper into the station's lower structures.

The outpost's underlevels consist of a tangled network of multi-layered maintenance corridors and conduits, originally designed for engineering drones. The Swarm seems to possess an innate awareness of these metallic labyrinths, flowing along the winding hallways and bypassing all deployed firepoints like black liquid metal surging through veins.

More troubling still, samples recovered from the front line reveal that some individuals have undergone "hardening" after repeated passage through spatial rifts — their surfaces have condensed into a dense crystalline armor layer, reducing the effectiveness of conventional machine gun rounds by nearly forty percent. This is not natural evolution; this is the Swarm learning and adapting in real time on the battlefield.

================================

[New Equipment: Concussion Missile Tower]

To counter the hardened Swarm, Engineering has urgently requisitioned a new automated defense unit from the starport: the Concussion Missile Tower.

Unlike the Machine Gun Tower's high-velocity armor-piercing rounds, the Missile Tower fires compact shaped-charge warheads. Upon impact, the warhead's metastable energy core releases instantaneously, generating a directed high-temperature plasma jet. This jet can strip away the Swarm's crystalline armor in milliseconds, exposing the liquid metal interior to a storm of high-energy particles.

However, the Missile Tower has a longer reload cycle and limited tracking capability against fast-moving targets. It cannot replace the Machine Gun Tower — it can only complement it, just as a scalpel cannot replace a tourniquet.

================================

[Tactical Upgrade Protocol]

In addition to new equipment, Engineering has declassified a technology rated "Level-Two Military Secret" by the Federation: the Tower Nano-Upgrade Protocol.

The principle is straightforward: inject additional nano-alloy materials and energy capacitors into an existing defense tower, allowing nanobots to reconstruct the fire array's acceleration tracks and power distribution networks internally. Upgraded Machine Gun Towers gain increased rate of fire and extended range; upgraded Missile Towers carry larger warhead payloads and wider blast radii.

Resources are limited. You must judge which positions merit the extra alloy and energy — upgrading the wrong location is just as fatal as failing to upgrade the right one.

================================

[Mission Objectives: KX-7 Deep Maintenance Corridors]

- Deploy mixed arrays of Machine Gun and Missile Towers along the winding alloy corridors
- Use the upgrade system to reinforce firepower density at critical corners
- Protect all 5 remaining Stellar Core Orbs
- If all orbs are lost, the lower reactor will melt down and the outpost will fall permanently

[Tip]
Click the "Missile Tower" button on the right panel to deploy. Right-click a built tower to open the upgrade menu. Prioritize Missile Tower placement at Swarm chokepoints to maximize shaped-charge kill efficiency.

================================

The sound at the end of the corridor is not wind. It is metal being torn apart.
level.3.name: Crossfire
level.3.subtitle: Combine MG and Missile towers
level.3.briefing:
Stellar Perimeter -- Mission Briefing

================================

[Background: The Starport Causeway]

The crisis at Outpost KX-7 is far from over, but the Resonance Locator has detected an even more troubling signal at the edge of the system — the Swarm is moving toward an abandoned starport.

The starport, designated "Skyhook-7," was an early Federation deep-space transfer hub connecting the outpost's main structure to an asteroid mining zone. A sixteen-hundred-meter alloy causeway stretches between the two massive installations, suspended above an endless void scattered with drifting rock fragments. The view is open, yet the deployable alloy decks are scattered like a chain dangling over an abyss.

Engineering initially assumed the Swarm would have no interest in derelict facilities. They were wrong. The Harmonic Trajectory Array shows that the Swarm's true target is not the starport itself, but the emergency energy relay station at the far end of the causeway — where three reserve Stellar Core Orbs are sealed. If the Swarm breaks through, they will simultaneously sever the energy supply lines to both the outpost and the mining zone.

================================

[New Threat: The Striker]

During the causeway engagement, the Resonance Locator captured an entirely new Swarm signal pattern for the first time.

This new variant was tentatively designated "Striker" by frontline observers. Unlike the slow, worm-like aggregates of the advance forces, Strikers manifest as unsettling liquid-streamlined structures that appear purpose-built for speed. Harmonic data indicates that Strikers travel nearly twice as fast as standard Swarm units, and excel at exploiting curved path segments for high-velocity flanking maneuvers that bypass frontal fire coverage.

More alarming still are intelligence analysis results: the Swarm has begun employing "mixed formation" tactics. Hardened units form an armored vanguard in the front, drawing and consuming defensive fire; Strikers then flank at high speed, targeting weak nodes in the fire coverage network. This is not instinct. This is tactics — a word that should not apply to non-sentient organisms.

================================

[Crossfire Network Doctrine]

Facing the threat of mixed formations, Federation Science Command proposed the "Crossfire Network" doctrine.

The core concept is not complex: leverage the Machine Gun Tower's mid-to-close range high-rate-of-fire interception capability to build a dense wall of projectiles along the front half of the path, slowing and weakening the Swarm vanguard; simultaneously deploy Missile Towers along the mid-to-rear sections, using their long-range precision strikes to surgically eliminate clustered targets that penetrate the projectile wall. The overlapping range zones of both tower types create a "kill band" — any Swarm entering this zone faces both armor-piercing round shredding and plasma jet incineration.

The key lies in timing and positioning. Deploy Missile Towers too early, and resources are wasted on sparse advance scouts; too late, and the Strikers have already crossed the intersection. You have one chance to turn this chain over the abyss into a garrote.

================================

[Mission Objectives: Skyhook-7 Starport Causeway]

- Deploy Machine Gun and Missile Towers along the narrow alloy causeway to build a crossfire network
- Machine Gun Towers handle mid-to-close range interception; Missile Towers handle mid-to-long range precision strikes
- Protect the 5 Stellar Core Orbs at the emergency energy relay station
- If all orbs are lost, both the outpost and the mining zone will lose power simultaneously

[Tip]
Machine Gun Towers are best placed ahead of path curves, maximizing hits during the brief slowdown as Swarm units turn; Missile Towers are best placed along straight mid-sections, giving warheads adequate time to lock onto clustered targets.

================================

The abyss does not actively devour anyone — unless you stand at the edge of the bridge with your back turned.
level.4.name: Mixed Assault
level.4.subtitle: Multiple rhythms per wave
level.4.briefing:
Stellar Perimeter -- Mission Briefing

================================

[Background: The Deep Furnace]

The battle for the KX-7 system has entered its final phase. Full-spectrum scans from the Resonance Locator point to a single coordinate — the Federation frontier's largest Stellar Core Orb refining facility, codenamed "Deep Furnace."

This installation is not a single structure, but an industrial cluster of eight large mining platforms interconnected by a complex network of alloy conduits. Each platform once served as a smelting and refining node for asteroid ore, and the channel network connecting them has now become a "highway" for Swarm advance. The facility's underground vaults contain enough Stellar Core Orb reserves to power the entire KX-7 system — the largest energy concentration the Swarm has discovered since the invasion began.

Federation Command has classified Deep Furnace as a "Non-Fallible" strategic priority. If this position is lost, not only will the outpost and starport lose power, but the entire system's defense network will collapse. Even more terrifying, if such vast energy reserves fall into Swarm hands, they could expand the KX-7 spatial rift to an unprecedented scale — and what pours through will no longer be advance scouts, but the Swarm's true main force.

================================

[Ultimate Threat: The Overseer]

While analyzing the harmonic signals at Deep Furnace's perimeter, Federation scientists discovered an unprecedented pattern: high intensity, regular fluctuations, with clear hierarchical structure. This did not resemble an individual. It resembled... a command center.

Engineering designated it the "Overseer." This represents the highest evolutionary form the Swarm has yet demonstrated. The Overseer does not directly charge into battle; instead, it hovers at the rear, broadcasting high-frequency subspace harmonic commands to surrounding Swarm units. These commands can precisely coordinate the action rhythms of different Swarm types — when advance scouts launch feints, when hardened units breach the front line, when Strikers execute flank attacks — all orchestrated by the Overseer's unified control.

Most dangerous is the Overseer's "vanguard escort" tactic: it personally leads an elite guard as the vanguard of a second wave, deliberately creating a brief time gap between the first and second waves. While defensive fire is absorbed by the first wave, the Overseer and its escort suddenly切入 from an unexpected node, driving straight for the energy core. This multi-rhythm, multi-wave precision coordination has surpassed any known biological behavior pattern.

One scientist proposed a chilling hypothesis over an encrypted channel: the Overseer may not be the Swarm's "commander" at all, but a more complete "projection" of higher-dimensional consciousness into lower-dimensional space. What we face may be merely the tip of an iceberg.

================================

[Full-Spectrum Defense Authorization]

Given Deep Furnace's strategic importance, Federation Command has lifted all restrictions on weapons system deployment.

In addition to Machine Gun and Missile Towers, you will also receive command authority over two critical systems:

Laser Tower: Fires a high-energy particle beam that inflicts sustained burn damage on individual targets. Its advantages include zero projectile delay and infinite penetration, making it particularly effective against fast-moving Strikers; its drawback is a brief charge time between shots and lower efficiency against clustered targets.

Slow Tower: Emits a special harmonic interference wave that disrupts the electromagnetic coupling within the Swarm's liquid metal structure, significantly reducing their movement speed. This tower inflicts no damage itself, but creates longer engagement windows for other fire units — against the Overseer's multi-rhythm assaults, every second of slowdown could determine whether the line holds or collapses.

================================

[Mission Objectives: Deep Furnace Mining Platform Cluster]

- Deploy full-spectrum defense arrays across the alloy conduits between the eight mining platforms
- Anticipate the Overseer's multi-rhythm assaults and maintain sufficient firepower at key nodes in advance
- Protect all Stellar Core Orbs in the Deep Furnace underground vaults
- If all orbs are lost, the KX-7 system will fall permanently and the rift will expand beyond containment

[Tip]
The Slow Tower and Laser Tower combination can effectively suppress Striker rushes; Missile Towers should prioritize the center of clusters to maximize shaped-charge blast coverage; when facing the Overseer's vanguard escort, reserve at least one upgraded Machine Gun Tower as the final line of defense.

================================

The tip of the iceberg has already breached the surface. And you stand on the deck, holding nothing but a lantern.

tutorial.welcome.ui:
Welcome to Outpost KX-7 Defense System
Please familiarize yourself with the interface
tutorial.hud.status: This shows your funds, base energy orbs, and current wave
tutorial.hud.time: This shows the elapsed game time
tutorial.hud.speed:
This shows the current game speed
Press 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 to set speed to 0.2x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, 5x
tutorial.hud.rightPanel: The right sidebar is the build panel, where you select and build towers
tutorial.hud.buildButtons: Click buttons to choose tower types, each with different attack characteristics
tutorial.hud.minimap: The minimap shows the entire battlefield and enemy routes
tutorial.hud.viewport:
This is the main battlefield viewport
Drag with mouse or use arrow keys to pan, scroll wheel to zoom
Settings also allow mouse-follow zoom
tutorial.mission.objective:
Your mission is to protect the base Stellar Core Orbs
The Void Swarm has no fixed form, but Resonance Locators have marked their paths
Destroy them before they reach the base, deploy your defenses now
tutorial.hud.nextwave: When ready, the next wave countdown will appear here
tutorial.hud.rangeButton:
Click here to show/hide all tower attack ranges
(Press F key to toggle all tower ranges)
tutorial.reward.kill: Eliminating the Swarm grants extra money rewards
tutorial.hud.towerMenu: Right-click on a built tower to open its menu for [Sell] or [Upgrade]
tutorial.world.spawn: The Swarm emerges from spatial rifts and advances toward the base
tutorial.world.base: The Stellar Core Orbs are here; the Swarm will try to seize them
tutorial.world.exit: The Swarm carrying orbs will flee into the rift; don't let them escape
tutorial.menu.start: Click 'Start Game' to enter level selection
tutorial.level.select: Click Level 1 to begin your defense journey
tutorial.briefing.start: After reading the briefing, click 'Start Mission' to enter the battlefield
tutorial.build.mg: Click here to select the Machine Gun tower
tutorial.place.tower: Click an empty spot on the map to place a tower
tutorial.start.wave: When ready, click here to start the wave
